entries.summary: New interaction-completeness skill — judgment-heavy 3-reviewer verification of interactive elements AND pages, modeled on editability-completeness
scenarios: Skill file exists and is well-formed
Skill mandates the three-reviewer converging structure
Skill covers both interactive elements and pages
acceptance_criteria: skills/interaction-completeness/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter (name: interaction-completeness, quoted description)
The skill body mandates 3 interaction-reviewer agents in parallel, a round-robin convergence step, a system-architect Round-3 robustness review, and a bounded multi-pass outer loop
The skill defines an element wiring-classification rubric (endpoint-backed, client-only, confirmed-stub, ambiguous) AND a page-classification rubric (live, placeholder, confirmed-stub)
The skill scope covers every interactive element and every page/screen/route; the element->endpoint trace and the genuine-user-driven test-authenticity audit are defined

entries.summary: New interaction-reviewer agent — spawned x3, independent, analysis-only; enumerates elements AND pages; mirrors editability-reviewer
scenarios: Agent file exists with valid frontmatter
Agent is analysis-only
acceptance_criteria: agents/interaction-reviewer.md has the 5 required frontmatter keys, model: opus, and every tool from the valid tool set
The agent's tools do NOT include Edit — it is analysis-only (classifies, traces, writes verdict/SR artifacts; never edits feature code)

The agent body covers independent enumeration of interactive elements AND pages, element wiring classification, page live/placeholder/confirmed-stub classification, element-to-endpoint trace, test-authenticity audit, hardcoded-dynamic-value detection, and round-robin convergence

entries.summary: An intentionally-inert element OR placeholder page is confirmed-stub ONLY with explicit user confirmation; unconfirmed ones are gaps
scenarios: Unconfirmed inert control is a gap
Confirmed stub is accepted without a user-flow test
Reviewer escalates rather than guessing
acceptance_criteria: skills/interaction-completeness/SKILL.md defines confirmed-stub — an inert element OR an intentional placeholder page — as requiring explicit user confirmation, with a structured escalation question
A confirmed stub is recorded in the converged interaction map and in the change's coverage-map.json confirmed_stubs[] list

An inert interactive element with no user confirmation is reported as an unwired-control gap; an unconfirmed placeholder page is reported as a placeholder-page gap; both are routed as solution requirements
A confirmed stub does not require a user-flow test but is tracked, not silently ignored

entries.summary: Evidence schema v5 to v6 — new hook-enforced ui_interaction_review field (pass/n/a/fail)
scenarios: Evidence missing the field is blocked
A fail value is blocked
An n/a value requires a note
A pass value with the field present validates
acceptance_criteria: hooks/review_evidence_schema.py SCHEMA_VERSION is 6 and ui_interaction_review is in REQUIRED_EVIDENCE_FIELDS
validate_evidence() returns a gap when ui_interaction_review is missing
validate_evidence() returns a gap when ui_interaction_review is 'fail'
validate_evidence() requires a non-empty ui_interaction_review_note when the value is 'n/a'
Both review-gate-task.py and teammate-idle-check.py enforce the field via the shared schema module with no drifted copy

entries.summary: test-completeness-verifier additionally flags vacuous-flow tests and cross-checks the interactivity inventory
scenarios: A navigate-and-assert test masquerading as a flow is flagged
An inventory element with no covering test is flagged
acceptance_criteria: agents/test-completeness-verifier.md instructs the agent to flag a Playwright test with page.goto + assertions but no genuine user-interaction calls (page.click/fill/selectOption/check/press/setInputFiles)
agents/test-completeness-verifier.md instructs the agent to cross-check evidence-listed Playwright tests against the interactivity inventory and flag uncovered elements
The verifier's verdict-JSON schema in the agent doc records the vacuous-flow and uncovered-element findings

entries.summary: Pipeline Phase 3+5 invoke the interaction-completeness team; playwright-user-flows / frontend / integration / team-spawning reference the v6 field + stub mechanism + placeholder detection
scenarios: Pipeline skill invokes the interaction-completeness team
The v6 evidence schema is documented
Frontend and integration agents emit the field
acceptance_criteria: skills/architect-team-pipeline/SKILL.md Phase 5 invokes the interaction-completeness team for in-scope frontend slices and Phase 3 names the ui_interaction_review field
skills/playwright-user-flows/SKILL.md references the ui_interaction_review field, the confirmed-stub mechanism, placeholder-page detection, and the interaction-completeness team
skills/team-spawning-and-review-gates/SKILL.md documents ui_interaction_review as a v6 field with pass/n/a/fail semantics and the n/a-note rule
agents/frontend.md and agents/integration.md instruct the agent to set ui_interaction_review and honor the confirmed-stub mechanism and the no-unconfirmed-placeholder-pages rule

entries.spec_requirement_id: ui-interaction-fidelity/Test coverage for the interaction-fidelity change
entries.summary: pytest coverage of the two new skills + agent registration, the v6 schema, hook enforcement, placeholder + dynamic-value rubrics, and the strengthened verifier
scenarios: New test files exist and pass
Full suite passes with no regression
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_interaction_completeness.py exists and asserts skill+agent registration, the skill's structural mandates, and the element + page classification rubrics
tests/test_ui_interaction_review.py exists and asserts the v6 field's required/valid/n-a-note behavior and SCHEMA_VERSION == 6

tests/test_dynamic_value_discovery.py exists and asserts the dynamic-value-discovery skill is well-formed, defines the context-classification rubric, and is referenced by the developer/architect/evaluator agents and skills
tests/test_skills.py EXPECTED_SKILLS includes interaction-completeness and dynamic-value-discovery; tests/test_agents.py EXPECTED_AGENTS includes interaction-reviewer
tests/test_review_gate_task.py and tests/test_teammate_idle_check.py evidence helpers are updated in lockstep for v6 and pass
python -m pytest -v passes from the repo root with all new tests and no regression in the pre-existing 496

entries.summary: README + CHANGELOG + maps + CLAUDE.md updated; version bump 0.9.18 to 0.9.19
scenarios: README documents the new gate
CHANGELOG and version are consistent
Maps reflect the new skills and agent
acceptance_criteria: README.md documents the interaction-completeness gate, the ui_interaction_review field, the confirmed-stub mechanism, placeholder-page detection, and dynamic-value discovery
CHANGELOG.md has a `## [0.9.19]` entry referencing the ui-interaction-fidelity requirements REQ-001..011
.claude-plugin/plugin.json and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json both report version 0.9.19

docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md documents 20 skills (incl. interaction-completeness and dynamic-value-discovery), 17 agents (incl. interaction-reviewer), and the evidence schema at v6; docs/INTEGRATION_MAP.md and CLAUDE.md are consistent

entries.summary: The interaction-completeness verification classifies every page live/placeholder/confirmed-stub; an unconfirmed placeholder wired where a live page belongs is a gap
scenarios: A route wired to a placeholder where a live page is specified is a gap
A user-confirmed placeholder page is accepted
The skill defines a placeholder-signal rubric
An ambiguous page escalates rather than being guessed
acceptance_criteria: skills/interaction-completeness/SKILL.md defines page/screen/route enumeration and the live/placeholder/confirmed-stub page-classification rubric

The skill defines a placeholder-signal rubric — component/file naming (Placeholder, ComingSoon, Stub, Mock, Demo, WIP), coming-soon/under-construction/lorem content, a data-driven page that makes no API calls, a near-empty route shell, a route-table entry pointing at a placeholder while the real component is specified-but-unwired

The verification cross-checks every page against the design / requirements / ROUTE_MAP.md; a route wired to a placeholder where a live page is specified is reported as a placeholder-page gap and routed as a solution requirement
agents/interaction-reviewer.md includes page enumeration, the live/placeholder/confirmed-stub classification, and the design/ROUTE_MAP cross-check in the reviewer's job
An unconfirmed placeholder page is a gap; a user-confirmed placeholder page is classified confirmed-stub and is not a gap; an ambiguous page escalates to the human

entries.summary: New dynamic-value-discovery skill — a cross-role discipline distinguishing static literals from sample-data standing in for dynamic, data-bound values
scenarios: Skill file exists and is well-formed
The skill classifies from context, not from the literal
Dynamic values must be bound to a data source
An ambiguous value escalates rather than being guessed
acceptance_criteria: skills/dynamic-value-discovery/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter (name: dynamic-value-discovery, quoted description) and is registered in tests/test_skills.py EXPECTED_SKILLS

The skill states that a value is classified static-vs-dynamic FROM CONTEXT (position, the value's nature, the requirements/design language) — the same literal can be static in one place and dynamic in another

The skill lists dynamic signals (person names, dates, currency, counts, statuses, a value in a record-detail view or a repeating list row, a greeting with a name) and static signals (nav labels, button text, headings, fixed helper text, brand strings)

The skill mandates that every value classified dynamic is bound to a named data source — never the design's hardcoded sample literal — and requires escalation to the human when a classification is genuinely ambiguous

entries.spec_requirement_id: ui-interaction-fidelity/Dynamic-value discovery wired into developer, architect, and evaluator
entries.summary: dynamic-value-discovery referenced by developer (frontend/backend), architect (system-architect/design-fidelity-mapping), evaluator (interaction-reviewer/interaction-completeness); hardcoded-dynamic-value gap kind
scenarios: Developer agents bind dynamic values
Architect classifies values at planning
Evaluator flags hardcoded dynamic values
acceptance_criteria: agents/frontend.md and agents/backend.md instruct the agent to apply dynamic-value-discovery — bind every dynamic value to its data source and never hardcode design sample data

agents/system-architect.md instructs the architect to consult dynamic-value-discovery when reviewing specs and designs, and skills/design-fidelity-mapping/SKILL.md has the DESIGN_MAP per-screen specs classify each value static/dynamic and name the data source

agents/interaction-reviewer.md and skills/interaction-completeness/SKILL.md instruct the evaluator to apply dynamic-value-discovery and report a hardcoded value the context shows should be dynamic as a hardcoded-dynamic-value gap
A hardcoded-dynamic-value gap is routed as a solution requirement and surfaces through the ui_interaction_review field

reuse_decisions.rationale: No existing component independently verifies interactive-element coverage, Playwright test authenticity, placeholder-vs-live pages, or hardcoded-vs-dynamic values. test-completeness-verifier is a mechanical sonnet grep (wrong kind of agent for judgment-heavy work); editability-completeness is attribute-granularity (wrong granularity). Two new skills (interaction-completeness, dynamic-value-discovery) and one new agent (interaction-reviewer) are a justified build-new.
reuse_decisions.codebase_map_anchor: docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md §4 Skills + Agents; the gap is named in skills/editability-completeness/SKILL.md ('the existing gates do not catch this')

reuse_decisions.rationale: interaction-completeness reuses the editability-completeness STRUCTURE verbatim — three reviewers in parallel, round-robin convergence, system-architect Round-3 robustness review, bounded multi-pass, gaps-as-solution-requirements; interaction-reviewer mirrors editability-reviewer (opus, analysis-only, x3). dynamic-value-discovery reuses the reuse-first-design PATTERN — a principle-skill consulted cross-role by the architect, developers, and evaluators, wired into existing agents with no new agent. Placeholder-page detection (REQ-009) reuses the same interaction-completeness skill, agent, reviewer team, confirmed-stub mechanism, and ui_interaction_review gate field — a page-level dimension, not a new component.
reuse_decisions.codebase_map_anchor: docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md §4 (editability-completeness skill + editability-reviewer agent; reuse-first-design skill)

reuse_decisions.rationale: The ui_interaction_review field is added to the existing shared hooks/review_evidence_schema.py module — the same path visual_fidelity_review (v0.5.0), test_completeness_review (v0.9.0), and integration_testing_review (v0.9.5) each took via a SCHEMA_VERSION bump; no new file, both hooks import the shared module. The verifier strengthening extends agents/test-completeness-verifier.md. The pipeline/discipline/dynamic-value wiring extends existing SKILL.md and agent files (architect-team-pipeline, playwright-user-flows, team-spawning-and-review-gates, design-fidelity-mapping; frontend, backend, integration, system-architect). Tests extend the existing pytest suite. Beyond the two new skills + one new agent above, there is no new command, no new hook, and no new third-party dependency.
reuse_decisions.codebase_map_anchor: docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md §4 Hooks (review_evidence_schema.py, evidence schema v5), §6 conventions, §8 navigation guide

notes.no_frontend: The architect-team plugin has zero frontend surface; Playwright user-flow tests, DESIGN_MAP, visual-fidelity, and editability reviews do not apply to THIS change. (The change is ABOUT Playwright/UI testing and design-fidelity discipline in target projects, but the plugin itself is verified structurally.)
notes.no_backend_api: The plugin has zero HTTP API surface; test discipline = pytest unit + structural. No requirement is layer=both, so no front-to-back integration criterion applies.
notes.run_mode: proposal-first — the orchestrator pauses after Phase 1 to present this proposal/design/specs/tasks/coverage-map package for user review before Phase 2 implementation begins. Revised twice: REQ-009 (placeholder-vs-live-page detection) and REQ-010/011 (dynamic-value discovery, cross-role) added per user feedback.
notes.teams: Phase 2 will finalize team boundaries. Provisional decomposition: interaction-discipline (REQ-001/002/003/009/010 — skills/interaction-completeness/SKILL.md, agents/interaction-reviewer.md, skills/dynamic-value-discovery/SKILL.md) and schema-verifier (REQ-004/005 — hooks/review_evidence_schema.py, agents/test-completeness-verifier.md) touch disjoint files and can run in parallel; wiring-tests-docs (REQ-006/007/008/011) depends on both and runs after — it owns the cross-role wiring edits and ALL test-file edits (incl. registering the new skills/agent), so each shared file has a single owner.
